title: crepis pulchra subsp. africana
intro: Crepis pulchra subsp. africana, also known as African hawk's beard, is a subspecies of the Crepis pulchra plant, which is a perennial herb belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its roots and leaves, which are used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Crepis pulchra subsp. africana include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. Crepis pulchra subsp. africana is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made from the plant's roots, leaves, or flowers.
